title: Wi-Fi Troubleshooting: Can't Connect to Corporate Network
text:
Q: What should I do if I can't connect to the corporate Wi-Fi network?
A: If you're having trouble connecting to the corporate Wi-Fi network, follow these steps: Windows: Step 1 - Ensure Wi-Fi is enabled on your device; check the physical switch or the function key (usually Fn + F2). Step 2 - Click on the Wi-Fi icon in the taskbar and select the corporate network. Step 3 - If prompted, enter your network credentials (username and password). Step 4 - If you still can't connect, right-click the Wi-Fi icon, select 'Troubleshoot problems', and follow the prompts. macOS: Step 1 - Click the Wi-Fi icon in the menu bar and ensure Wi-Fi is turned on. Step 2 - Select the corporate network from the list. Step 3 - If prompted, enter your network credentials (username and password). Step 4 - If the connection fails, open 'System Preferences', select 'Network', and click 'Assist me' for troubleshooting options. Additionally, check if other devices can connect to the same network; if not, there may be an issue with the network itself. If problems persist, contact IT support for further assistance.
